title: Stored XSS in Used Car Inquiry Form — CarsDirect.com
summary: Identified a stored Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in the used car inquiry submission workflow that resulted in JavaScript execution inside the dealer CRM through blind XSS callbacks.

findings.title: Stored XSS via Used Car Inquiry Form

timeline.summary: Blind XSS methodology adopted after WAF blocked direct payloads.
Received four webhook callbacks confirming execution inside dealer systems.
Retesting confirmed vulnerability had been remediated.

confidentiality.summary: Execution within internal CRM could expose sensitive dealer and customer information.

integrity.summary: Potential manipulation of internal workflows through trusted browser execution.

availability.summary: No meaningful impact on system availability.

privacy.summary: Customer inquiries and dealer interactions could be exposed.

regulatory.summary: Potential privacy obligations if customer information were accessed.

operational.summary: Compromise of dealer CRM sessions could affect sales operations.

lessons.summary: Blind XSS remains effective when direct reflection is blocked by a WAF.
Webhook callbacks provide definitive execution evidence.
Public forms often become trusted input for internal systems.
Demonstrating business impact strengthens vulnerability reports.
Time-boxing engagements helps maintain research efficiency.

seo.description: Case study covering responsible disclosure of a Stored Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability affecting an automotive lead management workflow.
